title: Octal escape sequences should be avoided
description: Octal escape sequences should not be usedAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellWhy is this an issue?More InfoOctal escape sequences in string literals have been deprecated since ECMAScript 5 and should not be used in modern JavaScript code.Many developers may not have experience with this format and may confuse it with the decimal notation.let message = "Copyright \251"; // NoncompliantThe better way to insert special characters is to use Unicode or hexadecimal escape sequences.let message1 = "Copyright \u00A9"; // unicode let message2 = "Copyright \xA9"; // hexadecimalAvailable In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIAvailable Since10.2Analyze
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

compliant:
let message1 = "Copyright \u00A9";  // unicode
let message2 = "Copyright \xA9";    // hexadecimal

checks.message: Avoid octal escape sequences in double-quoted strings - use Unicode escapes instead (e.g., \u00A9)
Avoid octal escape sequences in single-quoted strings - use Unicode escapes instead (e.g., \u00A9)
